methods.description: This method involves using drill sites to correlate seismic stratigraphy, which helps in identifying erosional surfaces and regional ties of sediment packages. This is crucial for developing new chronostratigraphies and understanding sedimentary successions.

steps.description: IODP Site U1521, drilled during Expedition 374 in 2018, and DSDP sites 272 and 273, drilled during Leg 28 in 1973, are used in this study to correlate the seismic stratigraphy.
steps.searchable_summary: use drill sites U1521, 272, and 273 for seismic stratigraphy correlation

methods.description: This method involves taking core and wireline logging measurements at various drill sites to analyze physical properties and correlate them with seismic data.

steps.description: Core and wireline (downhole) logging measurements were completed in the site to 650.1 m below seafloor (mbsf), above which depth average core recovery was 63%.
steps.searchable_summary: complete core and wireline logging measurements to 650.1 mbsf

steps.description: Wireline and core logs have been resampled to 5 m by applying a running average, to obtain records with vertical resolution approaching that of the seismic data while minimizing loss of variability.
steps.searchable_summary: resample wireline and core logs to 5 m using running average

methods.description: This method involves calculating density and porosity values from core and wireline logging data, which are essential for understanding sediment properties.

steps.description: The correlation between wireline downhole logged density and core logged wet bulk density has been calculated and used to correct the anomalously low wet bulk density values of DSDP sites 272 and 273 cores.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate correlation between wireline and core logged density and correct values

steps.description: Density values have been used to calculate porosities (φ) and acoustic impedance (I p ).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate porosities and acoustic impedance from density values

methods.description: This method involves measuring magnetic susceptibility, natural gamma radiation, and resistivity at Site U1521 to assess sediment properties.

steps.description: Magnetic susceptibility, natural gamma radiation, and resistivity have been measured at Site U1521 by both wireline downhole logging and multisensor core logging.
steps.searchable_summary: measure magnetic susceptibility, gamma radiation, and resistivity at Site U1521

methods.description: This method uses the formation microscanner (FMS)-sonic tool to provide high-resolution images of the resistivity-conductivity condition of the sediment surrounding the borehole.

steps.description: The formation microscanner (FMS)-sonic tool string was run at Site U1521.
steps.searchable_summary: run FMS-sonic tool at Site U1521
